included: DB cache and discovery runnable adapter matrix
plugin and template publish protocol enforcement
release evidence consumption and tag CI closure

claimBoundary.nonCommunityPolicy: This roadmap compares framework product and engineering capability only; community size, GitHub stars, downloads, contributor count, and brand awareness remain excluded from prioritization.
claimBoundary.transportParityPolicy: Do not claim Kitex, gRPC-Go, or go-zero transport parity until streaming contracts, resolver and balancer behavior, deadline and retry semantics, auth and tracing compatibility, benchmark budgets, and rollback evidence all have blocking gates.
claimBoundary.migrationProofPolicy: Migration claims must stay tied to runnable examples, smoke commands, generated diff categories, support bundles, compatibility caveats, and rollback actions before appearing in adopter-facing guidance.
claimBoundary.performanceClaimPolicy: Latency comparisons remain report-only unless benchmark trend evidence promotes a specific surface; allocation budgets may become blocking only through the existing regression ratchet gate.

dimensions.gap: After R7, the remaining non-community gap is converting broad adoption proof into smaller runnable contracts that map directly to framework migration decisions.
dimensions.todo: Publish the R8 roadmap and active aiflow batch with ordered task ids, acceptance gates, non-community claim boundaries, ignored runtime paths, and per-task commit checkpoints.

dimensions.gap: Top HTTP frameworks remain easier to evaluate because route groups, path parameters, binding, validation, middleware behavior, and error envelopes are familiar and immediately testable.
dimensions.todo: Add HTTP migration compatibility evidence for route groups, path/query/header/body binding, middleware ordering, stable error envelopes, OpenAPI schema output, and invalid-request smoke cases.

dimensions.gap: goctl and Kratos scaffolds are trusted because profiles, generated layouts, dependency placement, and repeat generation behavior are predictable across versions.
dimensions.todo: Add scaffold profile matrix evidence for REST, RPC, model, production service, import aliases, generated-only dependencies, repeat generation diffs, compile smoke, and rollback notes.

dimensions.gap: Kitex and gRPC-Go retain deeper streaming and transport confidence. gofly should keep transport claims conservative while making supported RPC boundaries testable.
dimensions.todo: Add RPC streaming boundary evidence for unary, server-stream, client-stream, bidi-stream, resolver updates, balancer routing, retry and deadline behavior, auth and tracing compatibility, and Kitex rollback notes.

dimensions.gap: Mature microservice frameworks make storage, cache, and discovery adapters feel owned through defaults, examples, observability, and fallback behavior.
dimensions.todo: Add runnable adapter matrix evidence for SQL transaction and outbox patterns, Redis and local cache behavior, cache invalidation, etcd, Kubernetes, Consul, Nacos, DNS, static discovery, observability, dependency ownership, and rollback.

dimensions.gap: Production-ready frameworks give adopters a reference topology they can run, observe, degrade, and roll back. gofly should deepen production-orders as proof rather than only an example.
dimensions.todo: Add production reference drill evidence for memory mode, Docker-backed mode, SQL outbox, MQ, cache, discovery, OpenTelemetry, K8s assets, rollback notes, and failure evidence.

dimensions.gap: Plugin and template ecosystems need a publishable protocol, not only examples. Permissions, digests, signatures, sources, compatibility, and failure isolation must be adoption-grade.
dimensions.todo: Add plugin and template publish protocol evidence for registry schema, manifest schema, digest, signature, permission minimization, source policy, template contract, malicious paths, and no partial writes.

dimensions.gap: Adopters trust mature frameworks when tag CI, artifacts, checksums, SBOM, Docker digest, vulnerability scans, provenance, and release evidence are one auditable chain.
dimensions.todo: Add release evidence and tag CI closure rows covering artifact producers, release snapshot, checksum, SBOM, Docker digest, Trivy, provenance attestation, required checks, publish/block decisions, and rollback.

dimensions.gap: HTTP and RPC leaders still have stronger performance reputations. gofly should make performance credibility evidence-driven without overstating parity.
dimensions.todo: Add performance ratchet evidence for REST router, path params, JSON binding, middleware, OpenAPI, governance overhead, RPC unary and stream surfaces, allocation budgets, report-only latency, and unsupported-surface handling.

dimensions.gap: Each R8 task must end with commit evidence, gate evidence, known risks, ignored runtime paths, and aiflow completion so the workflow remains auditable.
dimensions.todo: Record R8 convergence with per-task commit ids, executed gates, skipped or downgraded checks, runtime ignored paths, and next-round recommendations.
